direction_cf.label: Direction (CF)
direction_cf.summary: The trade direction picked by the Cornish-Fisher fat-tail-adjusted P(up).  When CF disagrees with the Gaussian drift, the rank is dampened automatically.
direction_cf.impact: Controls the SIGN of the effective Kelly rank.  Bulls Only / Bears Only filters use this field.

p_up_cf.label: P(up) CF
p_up_cf.summary: Cornish-Fisher fat-tail-adjusted probability that the return over the horizon is positive.  Accounts for realized skew and excess kurtosis of daily returns.
p_up_cf.interpretation: > 0.55 = strongly bullish, 0.45–0.55 = neutral, < 0.45 = bearish.  Reduces to plain Gaussian Φ(drift/σ) when higher moments are negligible.
p_up_cf.impact: Drives directional certainty and the effective Kelly rank — this is the canonical Future Mode ranking input.

p_up_gauss.label: P(up) Gaussian
p_up_gauss.summary: Naive Gaussian probability of upside — assumes normally distributed returns.  Shown for comparison against the CF-adjusted estimate.
p_up_gauss.interpretation: For fat-tailed distributions this typically over- or under-estimates P(up).  Watch the gap vs P(up) CF to spot left-tail risk.
p_up_gauss.impact: Not used in ranking — informational only.

drift_pct.label: Drift %
drift_pct.summary: Bayesian-blended expected return over the horizon.  Combines factor scores with horizon-scaled weights via posterior precision.
drift_pct.interpretation: Positive = bullish drift; magnitude in % of price.  Add jump-drift contribution for the full expected move.
drift_pct.impact: Primary input to effective Kelly rank; signed.

sigma_pct.label: Sigma %
sigma_pct.summary: Conditional volatility over the horizon.  Fast tier uses ATR-derived σ; GARCH tier uses fitted GARCH(1,1).
sigma_pct.interpretation: Higher σ = wider expected outcome range.  Used to scale VaR/CVaR and Kelly fraction.
sigma_pct.impact: Reduces effective Kelly rank quadratically through the Kelly fraction.

jump_drift_pct.label: + Jump drift
jump_drift_pct.summary: Expected drift contribution from rare large moves, derived from the jump-diffusion proxy (λ · μ_j · horizon).
jump_drift_pct.interpretation: Positive when historical jumps tilt upward; negative for chronic downward shocks.
jump_drift_pct.impact: Added directly to the drift before computing the effective Kelly rank.

var95_pct.label: VaR 95%
var95_pct.summary: Cornish-Fisher Value-at-Risk: the magnitude of the worst expected loss at 95% confidence over the horizon.
var95_pct.interpretation: A VaR95 of 2.1% means "we expect to lose ≤ 2.1% with 95% confidence."
var95_pct.impact: Risk-management context.  Not used in ranking but flags asymmetric downside.

cvar95_pct.label: CVaR 95%
cvar95_pct.summary: Conditional VaR / Expected Shortfall: the average loss in the worst 5% of outcomes.
cvar95_pct.interpretation: Always ≥ VaR.  Wider CVaR-VaR gap → fatter left tail.
cvar95_pct.impact: Risk diagnostic; supplements VaR with tail-mean info.

directional_certainty_cf.label: Certainty CF
directional_certainty_cf.interpretation: 0 = coin flip, 1 = absolute certainty in the direction implied by P(up)CF.
directional_certainty_cf.impact: Multiplied into the effective Kelly rank.  This is the magnitude of conviction Future Mode places on the trade.

kelly_fraction.label: Kelly Fraction
kelly_fraction.summary: Half-Kelly (Thorp 2006) position-sizing recommendation: half of drift/σ² per the log-utility optimum.
kelly_fraction.interpretation: Positive = long; negative = short.  Clamped to ±1 (no infinite leverage).
kelly_fraction.impact: Actionable — tells you what fraction of capital to allocate per the model.

effective_kelly_rank.label: Effective Kelly Rank
effective_kelly_rank.summary: Final Future Mode sort key: sign(CF direction) · |drift+jump_drift| · √precision · certainty_cf · regime_weight · agreement.
effective_kelly_rank.interpretation: Positive = long opportunity, negative = short opportunity.  Magnitude = conviction × expected move.
effective_kelly_rank.impact: The leaderboard sorts by this when Future Mode is on.  Bulls Only / Bears Only filter by sign.

regime_label.label: Regime
regime_label.summary: Hurst-derived market regime: trending (H ≥ 0.55), mean-reverting (H ≤ 0.45), or random-walk (0.45–0.55).
regime_label.interpretation: Trending regimes boost rank when direction agrees with the trend; mean-reverting regimes do the opposite.
regime_label.impact: Modulates the effective Kelly rank via `regime_weight`.

hurst_exponent.label: Hurst Exponent
hurst_exponent.summary: R/S Hurst exponent measuring long-memory in the return series.  Range (0, 1).
hurst_exponent.impact: Drives the regime classification and the regime weight applied to ranking.

realized_skew.label: Realized Skew
realized_skew.summary: Sample skewness of daily log returns (Fisher-Pearson, sample-bias corrected).
realized_skew.interpretation: Negative = left tail dominates (rare big losses); positive = right tail dominates.
realized_skew.impact: Feeds the Cornish-Fisher expansion → directly shapes P(up) CF.

realized_excess_kurt.label: Excess Kurtosis
realized_excess_kurt.summary: Sample excess kurtosis (Fisher form, normal = 0).  Measures tail thickness.
realized_excess_kurt.interpretation: > 1 = fatter-than-normal tails (typical for equities); > 5 = extremely fat-tailed.
realized_excess_kurt.impact: Feeds the Cornish-Fisher expansion → tightens VaR and shifts P(up) CF.

jump_intensity_per_day.label: Jump λ / day
jump_intensity_per_day.summary: Estimated daily jump intensity from the threshold-jump detector (3.5 · MAD).
jump_intensity_per_day.interpretation: 0 = no jumps in window; 0.05 = roughly one jump every 20 trading days.
jump_intensity_per_day.impact: Multiplied by mean jump return to produce the jump-drift overlay on each horizon.

jump_mean_return_pct.label: Jump Mean μ
jump_mean_return_pct.summary: Average return magnitude on jump days (%).
jump_mean_return_pct.interpretation: Negative = jumps tend to be crashes; positive = jumps tend to be rallies.
jump_mean_return_pct.impact: Sets the sign and scale of the jump-drift overlay.

ou_half_life_days.label: OU Half-Life
ou_half_life_days.summary: Ornstein-Uhlenbeck mean-reversion half-life fitted on log prices.  Days for a deviation to revert halfway.
ou_half_life_days.interpretation: 0 = no mean reversion (random walk); short half-life = strong mean reversion.
ou_half_life_days.impact: Informational — not currently fed into ranking but useful for short-horizon trade timing.

rv_har_sigma_pct.label: HAR-RV σ
rv_har_sigma_pct.summary: Corsi (2009) Heterogeneous AR realized-vol forecast for the next day, in %.
rv_har_sigma_pct.interpretation: Combines daily / weekly / monthly realized variance components.
rv_har_sigma_pct.impact: Reference σ shown for comparison against GARCH.  Not yet ranked-on directly.

rsv_upside_share.label: RSV+ Share
rsv_upside_share.interpretation: > 0.55 = volatility driven mostly by gains; < 0.45 = driven mostly by losses.
rsv_upside_share.impact: Component of the Lab composite multiplier.

egarch_leverage_gamma.label: EGARCH γ
egarch_leverage_gamma.summary: Asymmetry coefficient in the EGARCH(1,1) log-variance equation.  Captures whether negative shocks raise vol more than positive ones.
egarch_leverage_gamma.interpretation: γ < 0 = classic leverage effect (most equities).  γ ≈ 0 = symmetric.  γ > 0 = anti-leverage (rare).

garch_m_premium_bps_per_sigma.label: GARCH-M Premium
garch_m_premium_bps_per_sigma.summary: Expected return premium per unit of σ from a GARCH-in-Mean fit, in basis points per sigma.
garch_m_premium_bps_per_sigma.interpretation: Positive = market demands a positive risk premium on this asset.

permutation_entropy.label: Permutation Entropy
permutation_entropy.summary: Bandt-Pompe (2002) ordinal-pattern entropy of the return series, normalized to [0, 1].
permutation_entropy.interpretation: ≈ 1.0 = random / unpredictable; lower = more recurring patterns.
permutation_entropy.impact: Component of the Lab composite multiplier (low entropy boosts rank).

mahalanobis_outlier_z.label: Mahalanobis z
mahalanobis_outlier_z.summary: Multivariate distance of the latest trading day from the centroid of the prior days, in std units.
mahalanobis_outlier_z.impact: Component of the Lab composite multiplier (anomalous days dampen rank).

approximate_entropy.label: ApEn
approximate_entropy.summary: Pincus (1991) Approximate Entropy ApEn(2, 0.2σ).  Measures regularity / self-similarity.
approximate_entropy.interpretation: Low ApEn = regular, predictable.  High ApEn = irregular.
approximate_entropy.impact: Component of the Lab composite multiplier.

lab_qi_certainty.label: QI Certainty
lab_qi_certainty.summary: Quantum-inspired interference fusion of fast-tier and GARCH-tier P(up) values.  Constructive interference when tiers agree, destructive when they disagree.
lab_qi_certainty.interpretation: 0 = total tier disagreement; 1 = perfect agreement at high conviction.
lab_qi_certainty.impact: Currently informational; not blended into the composite multiplier (it operates per-horizon).

lab_rank_multiplier.label: Lab Rank Multiplier
lab_rank_multiplier.summary: Composite multiplier in [0.6, 1.4] derived from RSV share, permutation entropy, ApEn, Mahalanobis outlier, DFA α, SSA trend slope, and 2-state Vol HMM.
lab_rank_multiplier.interpretation: > 1 = lab signals favor this trade; < 1 = lab signals counsel caution.
lab_rank_multiplier.impact: When "Blend Lab into ranking" is ON, this multiplies the effective Kelly rank.

dfa_alpha.label: DFA α
dfa_alpha.summary: Detrended Fluctuation Analysis exponent (Peng et al. 1994).  Robust Hurst alternative that handles non-stationary trends.
dfa_alpha.interpretation: α ≈ 0.5 = random walk; α > 0.5 = persistent / trending; α < 0.5 = anti-persistent / mean-reverting.
dfa_alpha.impact: Contributes to the Lab composite multiplier.

ssa_trend_slope_pct_per_day.label: SSA Trend Slope
ssa_trend_slope_pct_per_day.summary: Slope of the leading Singular Spectrum Analysis component (smoothed trend) over the last 5 days, in %/day.
ssa_trend_slope_pct_per_day.interpretation: Positive = trend up; negative = trend down.  Robust to short-term noise.
ssa_trend_slope_pct_per_day.impact: Signed contributor to the Lab composite multiplier.

vol_hmm_p_stressed.label: Vol HMM P(stressed)
vol_hmm_p_stressed.summary: 2-state Hidden Markov Model probability that the LATEST day is in the stressed (high-vol) regime.
vol_hmm_p_stressed.interpretation: 0 = clearly in calm regime; 1 = clearly in stressed regime.
vol_hmm_p_stressed.impact: Stressed + sticky regime dampens the Lab composite multiplier.

vol_hmm_p_stay_stressed.label: Vol HMM P(stay stressed)
vol_hmm_p_stay_stressed.summary: Empirical transition probability that the stressed regime persists tomorrow.
vol_hmm_p_stay_stressed.interpretation: > 0.7 = sticky stressed regime; < 0.3 = quick mean-reversion to calm.
vol_hmm_p_stay_stressed.impact: Combined with P(stressed) drives the HMM contribution to the Lab multiplier.

strategy_vr5.label: Variance Ratio (5-day)
strategy_vr5.summary: Lo–MacKinlay (1988) variance ratio at q=5 days.  VR(q) = Var(q-sum) / [q · Var(1)].
strategy_vr5.interpretation: = 1 random walk; > 1.1 = positive autocorrelation (momentum); < 0.9 = mean reversion.
strategy_vr5.impact: Signed contributor to the Strategy composite multiplier.

strategy_vr22.label: Variance Ratio (22-day)
strategy_vr22.summary: Lo–MacKinlay variance ratio at q=22 days — captures monthly autocorrelation structure.
strategy_vr22.interpretation: Same scale as VR(5); equity literature commonly finds VR(22) near 1 with modest momentum/MR depending on regime.
strategy_vr22.impact: Signed contributor to the Strategy composite multiplier.

strategy_ar1.label: AR(1) Coefficient
strategy_ar1.summary: Lag-1 OLS regression coefficient.  Direct tomorrow-prediction slope.
strategy_ar1.interpretation: Bounded [-1, 1].  Positive = momentum; negative = mean reversion.  Magnitudes > 0.05 are unusual.
strategy_ar1.impact: Signed contributor to the Strategy composite multiplier.

strategy_mi_lag1.label: Mutual Info (lag-1)
strategy_mi_lag1.summary: Shannon mutual information I(r_t; r_{t-1}) in bits, using 10-bin discretisation.
strategy_mi_lag1.interpretation: 0 = independent; 0.05+ bits = meaningful nonlinear dependence.  Captures structure AR(1) misses.
strategy_mi_lag1.impact: Predictability contributor to the Strategy composite multiplier.

strategy_spectral_beta.label: Spectral Slope β
strategy_spectral_beta.summary: Power-spectrum slope from the FFT periodogram.  Power ∝ 1/fᵝ.
strategy_spectral_beta.interpretation: β ≈ 0 white noise; ≈ 1 pink noise (typical); β > 1.5 = strong long-memory structure.
strategy_spectral_beta.impact: Predictability contributor to the Strategy composite multiplier.

strategy_welch_cycle_days.label: Welch Cycle (days)
strategy_welch_cycle_days.summary: Period of the LARGEST Welch-periodogram peak that exceeds the noise floor by 2σ.
strategy_welch_cycle_days.interpretation: 0 = no dominant cycle detected; otherwise the dominant oscillation length in days.
strategy_welch_cycle_days.impact: Informational — useful for timing entries when a clear cycle exists.

strategy_rqa_determinism.label: RQA Determinism %
strategy_rqa_determinism.summary: Recurrence-plot fraction of points on diagonal lines of length ≥ 2.  Computed on a 64-day window.
strategy_rqa_determinism.impact: Predictability contributor to the Strategy composite multiplier.

strategy_lz_complexity.label: LZ Complexity
strategy_lz_complexity.summary: Lempel-Ziv 1976 complexity of the sign-discretised return sequence, normalised to [0, 1].
strategy_lz_complexity.impact: Inverse predictability contributor — high LZ dampens the Strategy multiplier.

strategy_emd_slope_pct.label: EMD IMF1 Slope
strategy_emd_slope_pct.summary: Slope of the first intrinsic-mode oscillation (high-freq component after stripping the rolling-mean trend), in %/day over the last 5 days.
strategy_emd_slope_pct.interpretation: Signed.  Positive = short-term momentum up; negative = short-term momentum down.
strategy_emd_slope_pct.impact: Signed contributor to the Strategy composite multiplier.

strategy_vol_regime_mom.label: Vol-Regime Momentum
strategy_vol_regime_mom.summary: log(σ_recent_5d / σ_long_30d).  Positive = volatility is rising relative to long-term; negative = cooling.
strategy_vol_regime_mom.interpretation: > 0.3 = stress entering; < -0.3 = stress exiting; near 0 = stable regime.
strategy_vol_regime_mom.impact: Cooling-vol regime contributes positively to the Strategy multiplier.

strategy_rank_multiplier.label: Strategy Rank Multiplier
strategy_rank_multiplier.summary: Composite multiplier in [0.6, 1.4] derived from VR(5), VR(22), AR(1), MI, spectral β, RQA determinism, LZ complexity, EMD slope, and vol-regime momentum.
strategy_rank_multiplier.interpretation: > 1 = strategy signals favor this trade; < 1 = strategy signals counsel caution.
strategy_rank_multiplier.impact: When "Blend Strategy into ranking" is ON, this multiplies the effective Kelly rank.
